zh.lifeSummary: 當Heinrich II von Holstein於1317年出生在德國史萊斯維-爾斯坦可萊斯 史萊斯維-福倫斯包格史萊斯維時，他的父親Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg24歲，母親Sophia of Werle22歲。他於1342年與Mathilde zur Lippe在德國諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 利普結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1384年11月16日在他的家鄉享年67歲，埋葬在德國史萊斯維-爾斯坦可萊斯 斯坦波格易。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Heinrich II von Holstein于1317年出生在德国史萊斯維-爾斯坦可萊斯 史萊斯維-福倫斯包格史萊斯維时，他的父亲Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg24岁，母亲Sophia of Werle22岁。他于1342年与Mathilde zur Lippe在德国諾爾德海-維斯特法倫可萊斯 利普结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1384年11月16日在他的家乡享年67岁，埋葬在德国史萊斯維-爾斯坦可萊斯 斯坦波格易。

nameMeanings.name: Holstein
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from the province of Holstein, long disputed between Germany and Denmark. This gets its name from holsten, the dative plural, originally used after a preposition, of holst, from Middle Low German holt-sate ‘dweller in the woods’ (from Middle Low German holt ‘wood’ + sate, sete ‘tenant’). The final syllable has been erroneously altered by analogy with North German Steen ‘stone’, which in South German and standard German has the form Stein.

variants.name: Hulstein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Albertus, Kees.
Dutch vowel variant of German   Holstein  .

variants.name: Hollstein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Hans, Heinz, Klaus, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
German: variant of   Holstein  , or perhaps a habitational name from Hollstein on the Werra River.

variants.name: Holst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Jorgen, Erik, Vibeke, Anders, Carsten, Ludvig, Sven.
North German and Scandinavian: habitational name for someone from Holstein (see Holstein ), a derivative of German Holtsate, Danish Holtsete ‘person from Holstein’.
Dutch (also Van Holst): habitational name for someone from any of several places so called, from holst, hulst ‘holly (forest)’. Compare Hulst .

variants.name: Holsten
meanings: North German: variant of   Holstein   or   Holst  .

nameMeanings.name: Heinrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Henry
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Ivo  , derived from the oblique case of the name.

en.lifeSummary: When Heinrich II von Holstein was born in 1317, in Schleswig, Schleswig-Flensburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Germany, his father, Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg, was 24 and his mother, Sophia of Werle, was 22. He married Mathilde zur Lippe in 1342, in Lippe, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 16 November 1384, in his hometown, at the age of 67, and was buried in Itzehoe, Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Heinrich II von Holstein est né en 1317, à Schleswig, Kreis Schleswig-Flensburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Allemagne, son père, Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Sophia of Werle, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde zur Lippe en 1342, à Kreis Lippe, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 16 novembre 1384, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Heinrich II von Holstein 1317 in Schleswig, Kreis Schleswig-Flensburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg 24 und seine Mutter Sophia of Werle 22. Er hat 1342 in Kreis Lippe, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Deutschland Mathilde zur Lippe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 16. November 1384 im Alter von 67 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich II von Holstein è nato il nel 1317 nel Schleswig, Kreis Schleswig-Flensburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Germania, il suo padre, Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Sophia of Werle, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde zur Lippe nel 1342, nel Kreis Lippe, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 16 novembre 1384, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Heinrich II von Holsteinが1317年にドイツスチレスウィグ-hオルステインクレイス スチレスウィグ-フレンスブルグスチレスウィグで生まれた時，父Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburgは24歳，母Sophia of Werleは22歳であった。1342年にドイツノードルヘイン-ウエストファレンクレイス リップでMathilde zur Lippeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1384年11月16日に故郷で67歳で死去し，ドイツスチレスウィグ-hオルステインクレイス ステインブルグイツェーhオにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Heinrich II von Holstein(이)가 1317년 독일 슐레스위그-홀스타인 크레이스 슐레스위그-플렌스버그 슐레스위그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Sophia of Werle(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1342년 독일 노르드하이-베스트파렌 크레이스 리프에서 Mathilde zur Lippe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1384년 11월 16일 그의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 슐레스위그-홀스타인 크레이스 스타인버그 잇츠호에 묻혔다.

pt.Gottorp Castle,Slesvig,Slesvig-Holstein,: Schleswig, Kreis Schleswig, Schleswig-Holstein, Preußen, Alemanha
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Heinrich II von Holstein nasceu em 1317, em Schleswig, Kreis Schleswig-Flensburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemanha, seu pai, Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Sophia of Werle, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde zur Lippe em 1342, em Kreis Lippe, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 16 de novembro de 1384, em sua cidade natal, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemanha.

ru.Schleswig, Schleswig, Schleswig-Holstein: щлесвиг, крис щлесвиг, щлесвиг-холстин, Пруссия, Германия
ru.Gottorp Castle,Slesvig,Slesvig-Holstein,: щлесвиг, крис щлесвиг, щлесвиг-холстин, Пруссия, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Heinrich II von Holstein родился в 1317 г. в местности щлесвиг, крис щлесвиг-фленсбург, щлесвиг-холстин, Германия, его отцу по имени Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Sophia of Werle, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde zur Lippe в 1342 г. в местности крис липп, нордраин-вестфеилен, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 16 ноября 1384 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности ицэо, крис стинбург, щлесвиг-холстин, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Heinrich II von Holstein nació en 1317, en Schleswig, Kreis Schleswig-Flensburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemania, su papá, Gerhard III of Holstein-Rendsburg, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Sophia of Werle, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Mathilde zur Lippe en 1342, en Kreis Lippe, Nordrhein-Westfalen, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de noviembre de 1384, en su pueblo, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Itzehoe, Kreis Steinburg, Schleswig-Holstein, Alemania.
